name: ECMWF Blue–White–Red Diverging (9)
context: Temperature anomalies, extended range, probability anomaly products
theme: Deep blue (negative anomaly) → white (neutral) → deep red (positive anomaly)
notes: Classic blue–white–red diverging palette. 9 levels symmetrically around white (neutral). Colours are RGB approximations from known palette positions.
author: ECMWF

name: ECMWF Blue–White–Red Diverging (20)
context: Higher-resolution diverging temperature / geopotential anomaly maps
theme: Deep blue → pale blue → white → pale red → deep red. 20-level high-resolution diverging.
notes: 20-level diverging blue–white–red. Used in EFI (Extreme Forecast Index) products. Colours are RGB approximations from known palette positions.
author: ECMWF

name: ECMWF Precipitation
context: Total precipitation, accumulated rain (medium-range), rain rate
theme: White (dry) → pale blue → blue–green → yellow–green → orange → dark red (heavy rain)
notes: White–pale blue–green–yellow–orange–dark red ramp. Standard ECMWF precipitation style. Colours are RGB approximations from known palette positions.
author: ECMWF

name: ECMWF Precipitation Blue–Orange (20)
context: Heavy precipitation events, ensemble precipitation, extreme precipitation
theme: Pale blue (trace) → deep blue → orange → pale yellow (extreme). 20-level precipitation.
notes: Pale blue → deep blue → orange → deep red. Used in ensemble precipitation products and SEEPS products. Colours are RGB approximations from known palette positions.
author: ECMWF

name: ECMWF Green–Blue Humidity (12)
context: Relative humidity at pressure levels (700, 850, 925 hPa)
theme: Pale yellow–green (dry) → teal → deep blue (saturated)
notes: Pale yellow–green–teal–blue ramp. Dry in yellows, moist in deep blues. Colours are RGB approximations from known palette positions.
author: ECMWF

name: ECMWF Cloud Cover Grey (20)
context: Total cloud cover, high/medium/low cloud cover
theme: White (clear sky) → progressively darker grey → near-black (overcast)
notes: White (clear sky) to dark grey (overcast). Simple perceptually uniform greyscale ramp. Colours are RGB approximations from known palette positions.
author: ECMWF

name: ECMWF Snow/Ice White–Blue (15)
context: Snow depth, snowfall accumulation, sea ice cover
theme: White (snow-free) → pale blue → deep navy (deep snow / full ice cover)
notes: White (snow-free) through pale blue to deep navy (deep snow/ice). Matches intuitive snow-colour perception. Colours are RGB approximations from known palette positions.
author: ECMWF

name: ECMWF Wave Height (10)
context: Significant wave height, total swell, wind wave height
theme: Pale blue (calm) → green → yellow → orange → deep red (extreme sea state)
notes: Pale blue (calm seas) through green–yellow–orange to deep red (extreme sea state). Ocean-specific colour ramp. Colours are RGB approximations from known palette positions.
author: ECMWF

name: ECMWF Sea Surface Temperature (17)
context: Sea surface temperature, lake temperature
theme: Deep purple (freezing) → blue → cyan → green → yellow → red (warm tropical SST)
notes: Deep purple (freezing) through blue, cyan, green, yellow to red (warm tropical SST). 17-level SST-specific palette. Colours are RGB approximations from known palette positions.
author: ECMWF

name: ECMWF Geopotential 500 hPa (11)
context: 500 hPa geopotential, thickness, 300 hPa height
theme: Deep blue (low geopotential) → white → deep red (high geopotential/ridge)
notes: Blue–white–red used for geopotential contour fill. Darker shades at extremes. Colours are RGB approximations from known palette positions.
author: ECMWF

name: ECMWF CAPE (10)
context: Convective available potential energy (CAPE), convective indices
theme: White (stable) → pale yellow → orange → dark red (extreme convective instability)
notes: White (stable) through yellow–orange to dark red (extreme instability). Used in medium-cape and ens-cape products. Colours are RGB approximations from known palette positions.
author: ECMWF

name: ECMWF EFI Blue–Red (10)
context: Extreme Forecast Index (EFI) for temperature, wind, precipitation, snow
theme: Deep blue (strong negative extreme) → pale blue → pale red → deep red (strong positive extreme)
notes: Diverging palette: blue = colder/drier/calmer than climatology; red = warmer/wetter/windier. Used across all EFI efi2web products. Colours are RGB approximations from known palette positions.
author: ECMWF

name: ECMWF Probability (10)
context: Probability of exceedance for any threshold (T2m, TP, wind, SWH)
theme: White (0%) → pale yellow → orange → deep red (100%)
notes: White–yellow–orange–red probability ramp. Low probability in pale yellow, high probability in deep red. Colours are RGB approximations from known palette positions.
author: ECMWF

name: ECMWF GEFF Fire Weather Index
context: Global ECMWF Fire Forecast (GEFF) — Fire Weather Index
theme: Dark green (low danger) → yellow → orange → red → deep purple (extreme FWI)
notes: Green (low fire danger) → yellow → orange → red → deep purple (extreme FWI). Used in GEFF ECMWF fire forecast products. Colours are RGB approximations from known palette positions.
author: ECMWF

name: ECMWF Aerosol Optical Depth Yellow–Brown (14)
context: Aerosol optical depth at 550 nm (CAMS), dust AOD, sulphate AOD
theme: Pale yellow (clean air) → warm tan → orange-brown → dark brown (heavy aerosol loading)
notes: Pale yellow → warm tan → orange-brown → dark brown. Intuitive for aerosol loading maps. Colours are RGB approximations from known palette positions.
author: ECMWF

name: ECMWF Visibility Red–White (10)
context: Visibility, fog probability, ceiling
theme: Deep red (fog / near-zero visibility) → orange → yellow → white (excellent visibility)
notes: Reversed: deep red (very poor visibility / fog) through orange–yellow to white (excellent visibility). Intuitive — more colour = more danger. Colours are RGB approximations from known palette positions.
author: ECMWF

name: ECMWF Soil Moisture (12)
context: Volumetric soil water layers 1–4, surface moisture
theme: Pale tan (dry) → warm brown → green → teal → blue (saturated / waterlogged)
notes: Pale tan (dry soil) → warm browns → green → teal → blue (saturated). Reflects soil colour transitions from arid to waterlogged. Colours are RGB approximations from known palette positions.
author: ECMWF

name: ThermClassic
context: Temperature anomalies, climate maps, departure from normal
theme: Cool blues diverging through neutral white to warm oranges
author: GeoPalettes

name: Drought Severity
context: Drought severity classification (US Drought Monitor scale D0–D4), PDSI, SPI, soil moisture deficit
theme: Pale cream-yellow (no drought) → warm oranges → brick reds → deep crimson → dark purple (exceptional drought)
notes: Colors re-optimised for perceptual uniformity (CIELAB ΔE76 CV=0.12) using equal-step LAB interpolation across three anchor segments: cream→yellow, yellow→red, red→dark purple. Preserves the original hue journey of John Nelson's palette.
author: John Nelson

name: NWS Temperature
context: Surface air temperature, weather forecast maps, NWS-style products
theme: Near-white polar → steel navy → frigid teal → lime → mellow yellows → orange-golds → scorching pink-reds
notes: Hard navy break at 0 °C acts as a visual freezing threshold divider. Inspired by National Weather Service temperature map conventions.
author: Emily Meriam

name: SST Pacific
context: Sea surface temperature, ocean heat content
theme: Deep ocean blues to tropical reds
author: GeoPalettes

name: Desert Heat
context: Surface temperature, heat stress, urban heat island
theme: Sand to scorched terracotta
author: GeoPalettes

name: Arctic Chill
context: Polar temperatures, permafrost, sea ice extent
theme: Ice white to deep polar blue
author: GeoPalettes

name: Rain Blues
context: Monthly/annual precipitation totals
theme: Dry pale to deep saturated blue
author: GeoPalettes

name: Drought Index
context: SPI, PDSI, drought monitoring; anomaly maps where negative = drought, positive = excess
theme: Wet teal through white to dry brown
author: GeoPalettes

name: Teal Cascade
context: Monthly precipitation, climatological normals
theme: Pale aqua mint to deep ocean teal
author: dominicroye

name: Lichen & Slate
context: Annual precipitation totals, humid climates
theme: Pale lichen green through steel blue to charcoal grey
author: dominicroye

name: Mist & Violet
context: Orographic precipitation, fog water input, montane rainfall
theme: White fog through icy blue to deep twilight violet
author: dominicroye

name: Tropical Burst
context: Tropical convective rainfall, ITCZ, monsoon peaks
theme: Ivory through vivid cyan and cobalt to deep magenta
author: dominicroye

name: Meadow & Dusk
context: Seasonal rainfall, transitional climate regimes
theme: Pale meadow green through cerulean blue to warm dusk salmon
author: dominicroye

name: Monsoon Burst
context: Heavy rainfall events, tropical convection
theme: Pale to vivid teal-green
author: GeoPalettes

name: Snowfall
context: Snowfall depth, snow water equivalent
theme: Near-white ice to deep indigo
author: GeoPalettes

name: Bathymetry
context: Ocean depth, seafloor topography
theme: Dark abyssal black to shallow turquoise
author: GeoPalettes

name: Hypsometric Tint
context: Land elevation, atlas cartography
theme: Classic cartographic green-to-brown-to-white
author: GeoPalettes
notes: Re-optimised for perceptual uniformity (CV=0.19) using equal-step LAB interpolation across four semantic anchors: lowland green → mid-elevation tan → bare-rock brown → alpine scree grey → summit white. Preserves the classic hypsometric tint convention for land-only elevation data.

name: Wind Speed
context: Surface wind speed, gust intensity
theme: Calm pale grey to intense violet
author: GeoPalettes

name: Storm Alert
context: Tropical cyclone intensity, severe weather warnings
theme: Advisory yellow through orange to extreme purple
author: GeoPalettes

name: RH Classic
context: Relative humidity drought–wet gradient; diverges from neutral (~50 %) toward arid amber and saturated violet
theme: Parched amber to saturated deep purple, diverging through near-white neutral
author: GeoPalettes

name: Soil Moisture
context: Volumetric soil water content, field capacity, wilting point
theme: Dry sandy beige to waterlogged dark brown
author: GeoPalettes

name: EVI Tropical
context: Enhanced Vegetation Index, tropical and subtropical forests
theme: Savanna gold to dense jungle deep green
author: GeoPalettes

name: AQI Standard
context: Air Quality Index following US EPA category thresholds
theme: Good green through hazardous maroon
notes: Category colors match US EPA AQI standard: Good (0–50), Moderate (51–100), USG (101–150), Unhealthy (151–200), Very Unhealthy (201–300), Hazardous (301–500).
author: GeoPalettes

name: PM2.5 Exposure
context: Fine particulate matter concentration, air pollution monitoring
theme: Clean white to smoky deep charcoal
author: GeoPalettes

name: Solar Rad
context: Shortwave radiation, insolation, solar energy potential
theme: Night black through deep blue to solar gold
author: GeoPalettes

name: Seismic Intensity
context: Earthquake intensity, Modified Mercalli Intensity scale, ground shaking hazard
theme: Imperceptible white to catastrophic deep red
author: GeoPalettes

name: pysteps Default
context: Radar-based precipitation nowcasting, real-time rainfall intensity monitoring
theme: Muted red-grey through magenta, blue, cyan, green, yellow to intense red
notes: Default colorscale of the pysteps precipitation nowcasting library. Designed for radar rainfall maps with logarithmic intensity thresholds. Uses 16 colors spanning five orders of magnitude (0.08–160 mm/h). CVD tested with colorblind (pypi.org/project/colorblind): fails protanopia — adjacent yellow-greens (#64FF00/#96FF00) collapse (ΔE ≈ 4).
author: pysteps developers

name: pysteps BOM-RF3
context: Australian Bureau of Meteorology radar rainfall display, operational weather forecasting
theme: White through blue, cyan-teal, yellow, orange to near-black red
notes: Colorscale used by the Australian Bureau of Meteorology for radar rainfall products (RF3). Starts from white (no rain) through cool blues and teals for light rain, then transitions sharply to warm yellows, oranges, and deep reds for heavy rainfall. CVD tested with colorblind (pypi.org/project/colorblind): fails both deuteranopia (white/#F5F5FF merge, ΔE ≈ 14; blue/#280000 confuse, ΔE ≈ 5) and protanopia (red/#C80000 collapse, ΔE ≈ 6).
author: pysteps developers (after Bureau of Meteorology, Australia)

name: pysteps STEPS-NL
context: KNMI (Netherlands) radar precipitation nowcasting, operational forecasting
theme: Light grey through sky-blue, dark blue, yellow, gold to dark red
notes: Colorscale from the STEPS-NL (Netherlands) precipitation nowcasting system. 10 discrete colors with a distinctive split between cool blues (light rain) and warm yellows/reds (heavy rain), separated at approximately 4 mm/h. CVD tested with colorblind (pypi.org/project/colorblind): fails protanopia — red/darkred (#FF0000/#8B0000) adjacent pair loses contrast (ΔE ≈ 12).
author: pysteps developers (after KNMI, Netherlands)

name: pysteps STEPS-BE
context: RMI (Belgium) radar precipitation nowcasting, operational forecasting
theme: Cyan through blue, green, yellow, orange, red to dark magenta
notes: Colorscale from the STEPS-BE (Belgium) precipitation nowcasting system. 14 discrete colors providing fine granularity from cyan through the full spectrum. Also supports reflectivity (dBZ) thresholds. CVD tested with colorblind (pypi.org/project/colorblind): fails both deuteranopia and protanopia — red/magenta (#FF0000/#FF00FF) collapse under both types (ΔE ≈ 1–2).
author: pysteps developers (after RMI, Belgium)

name: Batlow
context: General-purpose perceptually-uniform sequential for any geoscientific variable
theme: Midnight blue → forest green → sandy tan → pale pink. Perceptually uniform, low-contrast rainbow replacement.
author: Fabio Crameri
notes: Part of Scientific Colour Maps (Crameri 2018). Designed to be perceptually uniform, readable by people with colour vision deficiencies, and device-independent. Key control points only — interpolate for full gradient.

name: Oslo
context: Snow cover fraction, sea-ice extent, frost-day count, ice thickness
theme: Near-black → deep navy → steel blue → pale blue → near-white. Dark-to-light cold monotone.
author: Fabio Crameri
notes: Part of Scientific Colour Maps (Crameri 2018). Designed to be perceptually uniform, readable by people with colour vision deficiencies, and device-independent. Key control points only — interpolate for full gradient.

name: Lajolla
context: Aridity index, PDSI drought severity, vapor pressure deficit, fire danger index
theme: Cream → golden yellow → burnt orange → deep brown-black. Light-to-dark warm arid tones.
author: Fabio Crameri
notes: Part of Scientific Colour Maps (Crameri 2018). Designed to be perceptually uniform, readable by people with colour vision deficiencies, and device-independent. Key control points only — interpolate for full gradient.

name: Davos
context: Geopotential height (500 hPa), atmospheric pressure layers, deep-ocean pressure
theme: Deep navy → cobalt blue → greyish teal → pale silver → near-white. Cold, crisp vertical progression.
author: Fabio Crameri
notes: Part of Scientific Colour Maps (Crameri 2018). Designed to be perceptually uniform, readable by people with colour vision deficiencies, and device-independent. Key control points only — interpolate for full gradient.

name: Hawaii
context: Geological maps, lithology, chronostratigraphy, multi-class land surface
theme: Deep magenta → rust-orange → olive-yellow → soft green → pale cyan. Multi-hue spectral for discrete geology.
author: Fabio Crameri
notes: Part of Scientific Colour Maps (Crameri 2018). Designed to be perceptually uniform, readable by people with colour vision deficiencies, and device-independent. Key control points only — interpolate for full gradient.

name: Turku
context: Convective available potential energy, outgoing longwave radiation, surface emissivity
theme: Near-black → charcoal → warm grey → light tan → pale peach. Subtle warm monochrome for thermal intensity.
author: Fabio Crameri
notes: Part of Scientific Colour Maps (Crameri 2018). Designed to be perceptually uniform, readable by people with colour vision deficiencies, and device-independent. Key control points only — interpolate for full gradient.

name: GrayC
context: DEM hillshade relief, SAR backscatter, radar reflectivity, bathymetric shading
theme: Pure white → neutral greys → pure black. Perceptually linear grayscale, free of hue bias.
author: Fabio Crameri
notes: Part of Scientific Colour Maps (Crameri 2018). Designed to be perceptually uniform, readable by people with colour vision deficiencies, and device-independent. Key control points only — interpolate for full gradient.

name: Vik
context: Temperature anomaly, SST anomaly, heat wave intensity index, ENSO departure
theme: Deep navy → steel blue → pale grey → dusty rose → dark crimson. Blue–white–red diverging, cold/warm symmetric.
author: Fabio Crameri
notes: Part of Scientific Colour Maps (Crameri 2018). Designed to be perceptually uniform, readable by people with colour vision deficiencies, and device-independent. Key control points only — interpolate for full gradient.

name: Cork
range: e.g. ±2 PSU salinity or ±0.2 pH units
context: Ocean salinity anomaly, seawater pH, soil acidity, ion concentration gradient
theme: Deep purple-navy → steel blue → pale grey-blue → olive green → dark olive. Asymmetric cool-to-warm for ocean chemistry.
author: Fabio Crameri
notes: Part of Scientific Colour Maps (Crameri 2018). Designed to be perceptually uniform, readable by people with colour vision deficiencies, and device-independent. Key control points only — interpolate for full gradient.

name: Lisbon
context: Standardised Precipitation Index, Palmer Drought Index, river discharge anomaly
theme: Pale lavender-blue → royal blue → near-black → dark khaki → light yellow. Blue-dark-yellow for dry/wet contrast.
author: Fabio Crameri
notes: Part of Scientific Colour Maps (Crameri 2018). Designed to be perceptually uniform, readable by people with colour vision deficiencies, and device-independent. Key control points only — interpolate for full gradient.

name: Berlin
context: Relative vorticity, wind divergence, atmospheric circulation anomaly, Rossby wave activity
theme: Ice blue → cobalt → near-black → dark brick → salmon-red. Perceptual blue–black–red for dynamic meteorology.
author: Fabio Crameri
notes: Part of Scientific Colour Maps (Crameri 2018). Designed to be perceptually uniform, readable by people with colour vision deficiencies, and device-independent. Key control points only — interpolate for full gradient.

name: Broc
context: NDVI anomaly, GPP departure, LAI anomaly, greening/browning trends
theme: Deep indigo → slate blue → pale steel → sage green → dark olive. Muted cool–warm for subtle vegetation signal.
author: Fabio Crameri
notes: Part of Scientific Colour Maps (Crameri 2018). Designed to be perceptually uniform, readable by people with colour vision deficiencies, and device-independent. Key control points only — interpolate for full gradient.

name: Tofino
context: Sea surface height anomaly (altimetry), steric height, ocean eddy detection, convergence/divergence
theme: Pale periwinkle → steel blue → near-black → dark olive → lime-yellow. Blue-dark-green for oceanographic bipolar fields.
author: Fabio Crameri
notes: Part of Scientific Colour Maps (Crameri 2018). Designed to be perceptually uniform, readable by people with colour vision deficiencies, and device-independent. Key control points only — interpolate for full gradient.

name: VikO
context: Wind direction, ocean current bearing, wave propagation direction, topographic aspect
theme: Cyclic blue–dusty-rose wrap. Perceptually uniform cyclic palette: 0°=360° seamlessly.
author: Fabio Crameri
notes: Cyclic palette — first and last colors match seamlessly. Part of Scientific Colour Maps (Crameri 2018). Designed for directional data where 0°=360°. Key control points only — interpolate for full gradient.
